title: Stdlib: dot, cross, normalize, magnitude linalg builtins
analysis: All four linalg builtins (dot, cross, normalize, magnitude) are ALREADY fully implemented in eval_builtin() with correct dimensional tracking (lines 263-361 of stdlib/lib.rs). LSP completions already list all four (completion.rs line 86). Comprehensive unit tests exist (35 passing tests covering dimensionless, dimensioned, error cases, NaN/Inf, overflow). However, ALL existing tests use Value::Tensor inputs exclusively. The task specifies vec3(...) which produces Value::Vector. The implementation correctly handles Value::Vector (tensor_components_f64 accepts Vector, and wrapper-preserving logic returns Value::Vector from cross/normalize when inputs are Vector), but this behavior is completely untested. The gap is Value::Vector-specific test coverage: (1) dot with Vector inputs, (2) cross returning Value::Vector wrapper when both inputs are Vector, (3) normalize returning Value::Vector wrapper, (4) magnitude with Vector inputs. Adding these tests verifies the wrapper-preservation logic that distinguishes vectors from tensors at runtime — critical for downstream code (e.g., affine transforms where points get translation and vectors don't).

steps.description: Add dot tests with Value::Vector inputs: (1) dot_vector_orthogonal — dot(Vector([1,0,0]), Vector([0,1,0])) == 0.0 as Real, (2) dot_vector_dimensioned — dot(Vector([1m,0,0]), Vector([1N,0,0])) → Scalar{1.0, Length*Force}. These mirror the existing Tensor-based dot tests but use Value::Vector to verify tensor_components_f64 extracts correctly from Vector variant.
Verify dot Vector tests pass without implementation changes. tensor_components_f64 already matches Value::Vector, so these should pass. If any fail, fix the extraction logic.

Add cross tests with Value::Vector inputs: (1) cross_vector_returns_vector_wrapper — cross(Vector([1,0,0]), Vector([0,1,0])) returns Value::Vector([0,0,1]), NOT Value::Tensor — verifies wrapper preservation logic at line 312, (2) cross_vector_dimensioned — cross of dimensioned Vectors preserves Length*Force dimension on each component, (3) cross_2d_vector_returns_undef — cross of 2-element Value::Vector returns Undef.

Verify cross Vector tests pass. The wrapper logic (line 310-314) already returns Value::Vector when both inputs are Value::Vector. If the wrapper test fails, fix the match arm in the cross implementation.

Add normalize tests with Value::Vector inputs: (1) normalize_vector_returns_vector_wrapper — normalize(Vector([3,4,0])) returns Value::Vector([0.6,0.8,0.0]) with Real components, NOT Value::Tensor — verifies wrapper preservation at line 266, (2) normalize_zero_vector_input_returns_undef — normalize(Vector([0,0,0])) → Undef, (3) normalize_dimensioned_vector_input — normalize(Vector([3m,4m,0m])) → Value::Vector with dimensionless Real components.
Verify normalize Vector tests pass. The wrap function (line 265-268) already selects Value::Vector when input is Vector. If any wrapper test fails, fix the match arm.

Add magnitude tests with Value::Vector inputs: (1) magnitude_vector_3_4_0 — magnitude(Vector([3,4,0])) == Real(5.0), (2) magnitude_vector_dimensioned — magnitude(Vector([3mm,4mm,0])) == Scalar{0.005, LENGTH} — verifies dimension preservation through Vector path.
Verify magnitude Vector tests pass. tensor_components_f64 extracts from Value::Vector correctly and dimension is derived from first component. If any test fails, fix extraction logic.

design_decisions.decision: No implementation changes planned — only adding test coverage for Value::Vector inputs
design_decisions.rationale: All four builtins are already fully implemented with correct dimensional tracking. The tensor_components_f64 helper already handles Value::Vector extraction, and the wrapper-preservation logic (cross→Vector, normalize→Vector) is already coded. The gap is purely in test coverage: all 35 existing linalg tests use Value::Tensor, none exercise the Value::Vector code path.

design_decisions.decision: Tests explicitly assert Value::Vector wrapper type, not just component values
design_decisions.rationale: The critical behavioral difference between Tensor and Vector paths is wrapper preservation. cross(Vector, Vector) must return Value::Vector (not Value::Tensor), and normalize(Vector) must return Value::Vector. Tests must assert the outer enum variant, not just the inner f64 values, to catch regressions where someone might simplify the match arms.

design_decisions.decision: LSP completions verified as-is — no changes needed
design_decisions.rationale: BUILTIN_FUNCTIONS in completion.rs already includes dot, cross, normalize, magnitude at line 86. No modification required.

reuse.how: Already handles Value::Tensor | Value::Point | Value::Vector extraction — no changes needed for Vector support
Reuse for asserting scalar results from dot and magnitude
Same pattern used in existing dimensioned Tensor tests — reuse for dimensioned Vector tests

reuse.what: Existing test patterns for wrapper assertion (match + panic)
reuse.how: Same match-on-variant pattern but assert Value::Vector instead of Value::Tensor
